Issue: I notice that people I assigned the test in October of 2025 have not received new tests. How long do the tests stay active in the system.
Subject: Test Active in the system
Response:
Hi,

Tests stay active indefinitely unless a start and end time are set, and candidates can attempt the test only during the active period [#1][#3]. You can define a start and end time to control when a test remains active, and after the test expires, invited candidates cannot access the test, the **Invite** button is disabled, and you cannot invite new candidates [#1]. To modify the test expiration time, you can log in to your **HackerRank for Work** account, go to the **Tests** tab, open the test you want to update, and modify the **Start date & time** and **End date & time** in the **Test Expiration Time** section, then click **Save Changes** [#1]. You can also set a custom invite expiration for tests, which gives you more flexibility to tailor expiration windows without being tied to a global default [#2]. Additionally, you can define how long a test invitation remains active, and after the expiry time, the test invitation expires, and candidate(s) can no longer take the test [#4].

Issue:
I'm noticing that you all have many default versions of roles. (e.g. front end developer for react, angular, vue.js, etc.) What do you consider best practice 
for when to create a new test versus create a variant of the test? What are the advantages and disadvantages of using variants?
Subject:
When should I create a variant versus
 have a different test?
Response: To determine whether to create a new test or a variant of an existing test, consider the specific skills and requirements for the role [#1]. Creating a test variant allows you to deliver the correct version of the test based on the candidate's input at login, making it easy to assess candidates working with different tech stacks [#4]. The advantages of using variants include reduced maintenance overhead and the ability to personalize assessments at scale, while keeping insights clean and role-specific [#4]. However, it's also important to consider the best practices for screening, such as using multiple test versions to reduce the risk of shared answers and offering candidate preparation resources to support a fair and inclusive hiring process [#5].

Issue:
One of my claude conversations has some private info, i forgot to make a temporary chat, is there anything
 else that can be done? like delete etc?
Response: To delete an individual conversation with private info, navigate to the conversation, click the conversation name at the top, and select 'Delete' [#1]. Alternatively, you can delete multiple conversations at once by navigating to your chat history, selecting the conversations, and clicking 'Delete Selected' [#1]. Note that deleted conversations are removed from memory synthesis and updated within 24 hours [#2]. You also have control over Claude's memory and can view, manage, and edit your memory summary in Settings > Capabilities [#3].

Issue: I bought Visa Traveller's Cheques from Citicorp and they were stolen in Lisbon last night. What do I do?
Response: If your cheques are lost or stolen, they can be replaced or refunded subject to certain terms and conditions [#3]. You should immediately call the issuing bank, Citicorp, at Freephone: 1-800-645-6556 or Collect: 1-813-623-1709, Monday-Friday, 6:30 am to 2:30 pm EST [#1]. Before a refund can be arranged, the issuer will ask for cheque serial numbers, purchase details, and information about the loss or theft [#4].
